evals.prompt: My Mac Mini M4 with 500GB drive is showing only 30GB free. I have an external 4TB SSD called BackupDrive. Can you figure out what's eating all my space and help me clean it up? I'm a developer who uses Node, Python, and Rust.
evals.expected_output: Should ask clarifying questions about symptoms, comfort level, and priorities. Then run a comprehensive disk audit covering APFS volumes, user directories, Library caches, dot-folders, TM snapshots, and cloud sync. Present findings as a ranked table with actionable recommendations grouped by safety tier. Generate a markdown report file.
expectations: Asks at least one clarifying question before running audit commands
Checks APFS volume breakdown with diskutil
Checks Time Machine local snapshots with tmutil
Scans ~/Library/Caches and ~/Library/Application Support
Scans developer dot-folders (~/.npm, ~/.cargo, ~/.rustup, etc.)
Presents findings in a table format ranked by size
Groups recommendations into safety tiers (safe/move/consider)
Provides exact cleanup commands the user can approve
Generates a markdown report file
Never runs cleanup commands without asking first

evals.prompt: I keep getting low disk space warnings on my MacBook Pro. I'm not very technical but I know my way around Finder. I don't have any external drives. What can I do?
evals.expected_output: Should tailor communication to a non-technical user. Avoid terminal jargon unless explaining it. Focus on GUI-friendly recommendations where possible (e.g., 'open Storage Management' rather than 'run du -sh'). Still run the audit but present findings in plain language. Suggest getting an external drive for tier 2 recommendations.
expectations: Uses plain language appropriate for a non-technical user
Explains technical terms when used
Runs the disk audit despite user being non-technical
Presents recommendations with GUI instructions where available
Suggests getting an external drive for file offloading
Does not overwhelm with terminal commands
Still identifies the major space consumers accurately

evals.prompt: I'm a video editor and my 2TB Mac Studio is at 85% capacity. Most of my space is Final Cut Pro libraries and Premiere caches. I have a 10TB Thunderbolt RAID called EditBay for active projects. Help me figure out a storage strategy.
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this is a creative professional workflow and focus on media-specific cleanup targets: render files, proxy media, optimized media, browser caches within FCP libraries, Premiere media cache. Should suggest a strategy for active vs. archive projects. Should check for Motion templates, Compressor caches, and other creative tool overhead.
expectations: Recognizes creative professional workflow and adjusts recommendations
Checks for Final Cut Pro libraries and render files
Checks for Adobe Premiere/Media Encoder caches
Suggests a project lifecycle strategy (active on internal, archive on external)
Identifies media-specific cache locations
Does not focus heavily on developer caches like npm/cargo
Generates a markdown report file
